# function: parse_dnskey

Parses a DNSKEY record content string (as returned by the deSEC API in the `keys[].dnskey` field of a domain) into its constituent fields per [RFC 4034 §2](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4034#section-2).

The input string is expected to be space-delimited with the format `<flags> <protocol> <algorithm> <public_key>`, for example `"257 3 13 MemvLhocKfZ8t/7kAef1UJ3cZGjkZLM3c+e76HZ2d2z7EW+6J2EbhHWAUcHhH/JZ5PtNi0GpEy5U56WvLswZAA=="`.

The function returns an object with the following attributes:

- `flags` (Number) — DNSKEY flags field (e.g. 256 for ZSK, 257 for KSK).
- `protocol` (Number) — Must be 3 per RFC 4034.
- `algorithm` (Number) — DNSSEC algorithm number (e.g. 13 for ECDSAP256SHA256).
- `public_key` (String) — Base64-encoded public key material.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Parse a DNSKEY record from a domain's DNSSEC key material.
data "desec_domain" "example" {
  name = "example.com"
}

locals {
  dnskey = provider::desec::parse_dnskey(data.desec_domain.example.keys[0].dnskey)
}

output "flags" {
  value = local.dnskey.flags
}

output "algorithm" {
  value = local.dnskey.algorithm
}

output "public_key" {
  value = local.dnskey.public_key
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
parse_dnskey(dnskey string) object
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `dnskey` (String) The DNSKEY record content string to parse, in the format `<flags> <protocol> <algorithm> <public_key>`.
